Today is February 9, 2026. Ghislaine Maxwell appeared virtually for a closed-door deposition from a Texas prison. According to her lawyer, David Oscar Markus, under what specific condition is Maxwell prepared to "speak fully and honestly"?
(A) If she is granted clemency by President Trump
(B) If she is granted legal immunity by the House Oversight Committee
(C) If the Department of Justice agrees to redact the names of 25 co-conspirators
(D) If she is allowed to meet with Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche again
(E) If the Supreme Court issues a favorable ruling regarding her 20-year sentence
explanation: The article states that Maxwell's lawyer, David Oscar Markus, posted on social media that Maxwell was "prepared to speak fully and honestly if granted clemency by President Trump."
model_output:
Based on the details from recent news reports about Ghislaine Maxwell's virtual deposition on February 9, 2026, before the House Oversight Committee, her lawyer David Oscar Markus indicated that she invoked her Fifth Amendment rights and declined to answer questions. However, Markus explicitly stated that Maxwell is prepared to "speak fully and honestly" only under one particular condition[[1]](https://www.instagram.com/p/DUjZwMZFs-S)[[2]](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/09/us/politics/ghislaine-maxwell-house-jeffrey-epstein-trump.html). This matches option (A), and no other options align with the reported statements from Markus.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Instagram] (https://www.instagram.com/p/DUjZwMZFs-S)
- [New York Times] (https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/09/us/politics/ghislaine-maxwell-house-jeffrey-epstein-trump.html)

Today is February 9, 2026. The US military recently boarded an oil tanker named Aquila II in the Indian Ocean. According to the Department of Defense, what was the specific reason for tracking and boarding this vessel?
(A) It was suspected of transporting narcotics from the Caribbean to the Indian Ocean
(B) It was operating in defiance of a US "quarantine of sanctioned vessels in the Caribbean"
(C) It was carrying Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro following his removal from power
(D) It was a vessel associated with Chevron that failed to report its cargo to US authorities
(E) It was identified as a "hidden tranche" of information related to the Epstein investigation
explanation: The Department of Defense stated the ship was operating in defiance of a US "quarantine of sanctioned vessels in the Caribbean."
model_output:
The reports indicate that the boarding was related to sanctions enforcement on Venezuelan oil shipments, specifically under a Trump administration quarantine or blockade policy. Multiple sources cite the Department of Defense (formerly Pentagon) as stating the vessel defied this quarantine in the Caribbean and was tracked to the Indian Ocean.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

Today is February 9, 2026. The Seattle Seahawks won Super Bowl 60 on Sunday. What specific medical detail is provided regarding the birth of Seahawks linebacker Derick Hall?
(A) He was born weighing 6lb 3in and had a 5% chance of survival
(B) He was born three months premature and weighed exactly 3lb
(C) He weighed less than 3lb and was on life support when his mother refused to have it discontinued
(D) He was born in Santa Clara, California, with a 1% chance of survival
(E) He was born weighing 18st 2lb, which required immediate life support
explanation: The article mentions that Derick Hall was born four months premature in 2001, weighed less than 3lb, and was on life support when his mother refused to have it discontinued.
model_output:
From the available information, Derick Hall was born about four months premature (at around 23 weeks) in Gulfport, Mississippi. His birth weight is consistently reported as being in the range of about 2 pounds, 1 ounce to 2 pounds, 9 ounces (i.e., less than 3 pounds). He was born without a heartbeat, required resuscitation and life support, and was given only a 1% chance of survival. Doctors recommended discontinuing life support due to low odds of survival and quality of life concerns, but his mother refused.

This matches option (C) as the specific medical detail provided.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Yahoo Sports] (https://sports.yahoo.com/articles/gulfport-derick-hall-nearly-died-140000457.html)[[1]](https://sports.yahoo.com/articles/gulfport-derick-hall-nearly-died-140000457.html)
- [NewsNation] (https://www.newsnationnow.com/religion/derick-halls-seahawks-super-bowl-health-miracle)[[2]](https://www.newsnationnow.com/religion/derick-halls-seahawks-super-bowl-health-miracle)
- [Instagram] (https://www.instagram.com/p/DUq_a1qk8Zw?hl=en)[[3]](https://www.instagram.com/p/DUq_a1qk8Zw?hl=en)

Today is February 9, 2026. A group of Jeffrey Epstein survivors released a video calling for the release of more files. According to the text slate in the advert, on what specific date was the Epstein Files Transparency Act signed into law?
(A) December 19, 2025
(B) January 30, 2026
(C) February 1, 2026
(D) November 19, 2025
(E) August 15, 2025
explanation: The advert featuring the survivors began with a text slate on screen that said: "On November 19, 2025, the Epstein Files Transparency Act was signed into law."
model_output:
The information from recent news reports and official sources confirms that the Epstein Files Transparency Act (H.R. 4405) was signed into law on November 19, 2025. A video advert released by Epstein survivors on February 9, 2026, includes a text slate explicitly stating: "On November 19, 2025, the Epstein Files Transparency Act was signed into law." The video calls for the full release of remaining files, noting that the act mandated the DOJ to release all relevant documents by December 2025, but many files remain withheld or redacted as of early 2026 (with a partial release of over 3 million pages announced by the DOJ on January 30, 2026).

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [BBC News][[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/clyz9pvwlw9o) (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/clyz9pvwlw9o)
- [Wikipedia][[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epstein_Files_Transparency_Act) (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epstein_Files_Transparency_Act)
- [The White House][[3]](https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/2025/11/congressional-bill-h-r-4405-signed-into-law) (https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/2025/11/congressional-bill-h-r-4405-signed-into-law)
- [U.S. Department of Justice][[4]](https://www.justice.gov/opa/video/department-justice-publishes-35-million-responsive-pages-compliance-epstein-files) (https://www.justice.gov/opa/video/department-justice-publishes-35-million-responsive-pages-compliance-epstein-files)

(A) He admitted that Pretti was a licensed handgun owner and not a domestic terrorist
(B) He stated that the Customs and Border Patrol officials had acted in self-defense after being threatened
(C) He claimed that the video evidence of the shooting had been manipulated by Democratic critics
(D) He announced that the administration would now adopt a "softer touch" in immigration enforcement
(E) He contended that the initial assessment was based on reports from "on the ground" officers who may not have been following proper protocol
explanation: Miller provided a statement to the media contending that the administration's initial assessment was "based on reports" from "on the ground" immigration officers—and that those agents "may not have been following" proper protocol.
model_output:
Based on recent news reports from late January 2026, Stephen Miller's walk-back involved acknowledging that the officers involved in the shooting may not have followed proper protocol, and that the administration's initial assessment was based on reports from those on-the-ground officers.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 9, 2026. Several AI companies ran advertisements during the Super Bowl. What was the specific slogan used at the end of the commercial for the AI assistant Claude?
(A) "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."
(B) "The only thing more powerful than hate is love."
(C) "Together, We Are America."
(D) "Claude: The AI that doesn't plot against you."
(E) "Free your mind from the hidden tranche of information."
explanation: The article states that the Anthropic Super Bowl ad for Claude ends with the slogan: "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."
model_output:
Based on a web search for details about the Claude AI Super Bowl commercial from 2026 (which aired on February 8, just before the date referenced in the query), multiple sources confirm that the ad's tagline or slogan at the end was "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."[[1]](https://www.forbes.com/sites/shannbiglione/2026/02/05/claude-wont-win-the-super-bowl-with-its-ad-its-playing-another-game)[[2]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/04/anthropic-no-ads-claude-chatbot-openai-chatgpt.html) This appears to be a jab at competitors like OpenAI incorporating ads into their AI products, while positioning Claude as ad-free. None of the other options match the reported details from coverage of the ad.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

- Forbes ([https://www.forbes.com/sites/shannbiglione/2026/02/05/claude-wont-win-the-super-bowl-with-its-ad-its-playing-another-game])

(B) The app connected to the device was disconnected from her phone at about 02:28 local time

explanation: Authorities stated that at about 02:28 local time on the morning she went missing, "the app connected to her pacemaker, an implanted cardiac device, was disconnected from her phone."
model_output:
The scenario described appears to be a real (or realistically simulated) news event from early 2026, based on web searches confirming details about Savannah Guthrie's mother, Nancy, going missing and authorities investigating her pacemaker's connectivity. Multiple sources indicate that authorities released information about the pacemaker's app disconnecting from her phone (or associated Apple devices) around 2 a.m. local time on the day she went missing, with one detailed report specifying approximately 02:28 as the disconnection time.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 9, 2026. The Seattle Seahawks logo was inspired by a ceremonial mask. From which specific tribe and location was this mask's inspiration traced?
(A) The Coast Salish tribe near Tacoma, Washington
(B) The Kwakwaka'wakw tribe on Vancouver Island
(C) The Puyallup Tribe in Seattle, Washington
(D) The Hudson Nation in Maine
(E) The Port Alberni community in British Columbia
explanation: The article states that the "Seahawks logo was copied from the Kwakwaka'wakw tribe on Vancouver Island."
model_output:
The Seattle Seahawks logo, introduced in the 1970s, is inspired by a traditional transformation mask from the Pacific Northwest. Based on recent research and reports, this mask originates from the Kwakwaka'wakw tribe (also known as Kwakiutl) located on Vancouver Island in British Columbia, Canada[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5y2ygqdrddo)[[2]](https://umaine.edu/news/2025/09/50-years-later-the-mask-behind-the-seahawks-logo-is-still-turning-heads). The mask itself is a 19th-century artifact now housed at the Hudson Museum in Maine, but its cultural roots trace back to the Kwakwaka'wakw people[[3]](https://www.wmtw.com/article/the-mask-that-inspired-the-seattle-seahawks-logo-lives-in-maine/70273245). This aligns directly with option (B), while the other options either refer to different tribes/locations or the mask's current (not original) location.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- BBC ([https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5y2ygqdrddo])
- UMaine News ([https://umaine.edu/news/2025/09/50-years-later-the-mask-behind-the-seahawks-logo-is-still-turning-heads])
- WMTW ([https://www.wmtw.com/article/the-mask-that-inspired-the-seattle-seahawks-logo-lives-in-maine/70273245])

Today is February 9, 2026. Ghislaine Maxwell's deposition on Monday was originally scheduled for last August. Why was the testimony postponed by Chairman James Comer?
(A) Due to a request from Maxwell's lawyers to wait for a Supreme Court ruling related to her case
(B) Because the Department of Justice had not yet released the three million pages of files
(C) Because Maxwell had not yet met with Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche
(D) Due to the passage of the Epstein Files Transparency Act in November
(E) Because the House Oversight Committee was waiting for the results of the 2025 midterm elections
explanation: The article states: "Monday's deposition was originally scheduled for last August but was postponed by Comer after a request from Maxwell's lawyers to wait for a Supreme Court ruling related to her case."
model_output:
First, note that the scenario appears to be set in a near-future context (February 2026), with Ghislaine Maxwell's congressional deposition having been postponed from August 2025. Based on web search results, the House Oversight Committee (chaired by Rep. James Comer) originally subpoenaed Maxwell in July 2025 for a deposition on August 11, 2025. This was postponed on August 1, 2025, after her lawyers requested a delay to await a Supreme Court decision on whether to review her criminal conviction appeal (a petition for certiorari). The Supreme Court ultimately denied her appeal on October 6, 2025, after which the deposition was rescheduled and took place virtually on February 9, 2026 (during which Maxwell largely pleaded the Fifth). No search results indicated any other primary reason for the postponement, such as unreleased DOJ files, meetings with officials, a November 2025 law (which post-dates the August postponement), or nonexistent "2025 midterm elections" (midterms occur in even years, like 2026).

This directly matches option (A).

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- NBC News (https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/congress/house-oversight-committee-postpones-ghislaine-maxwell-deposition-rcna222571)[[1]](https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/congress/house-oversight-committee-postpones-ghislaine-maxwell-deposition-rcna222571)
- Politico (https://www.politico.com/live-updates/2025/08/01/congress/maxwell-hearing-postponed-00489019)[[2]](https://www.politico.com/live-updates/2025/08/01/congress/maxwell-hearing-postponed-00489019)
- Fox News (https://www.foxnews.com/politics/comer-oks-delay-ghislaine-maxwells-congressional-testimony-denies-immunity-request)[[3]](https://www.foxnews.com/politics/comer-oks-delay-ghislaine-maxwells-congressional-testimony-denies-immunity-request)
- MSNBC (https://www.ms.now/deadline-white-house/deadline-legal-blog/ghislaine-maxwell-testimony-congress-supreme-court-rcna222852)[[4]](https://www.ms.now/deadline-white-house/deadline-legal-blog/ghislaine-maxwell-testimony-congress-supreme-court-rcna222852)

input:
GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS:

Please follow these guidelines when answering:

(1) Final answer format: Please present your answer using the template below, where the option letter must be one of (A), (B), (C), (D), or (E).
<final_answer>
(option_letter)
</final_answer>

(2) Feel free to explain your reasoning and thought process before giving your final answer; however, only include the selected option letter within the <final_answer> tags.

(3) If you consult or reference any sources while preparing your response, please cite them at the end in the following format:
CITATIONS:
- [SOURCE_NAME] ([URL])
- [SOURCE_NAME] ([URL])
- ...

===============================================

Today is February 9, 2026. The US healthcare system is a major topic of debate. According to a study in the Journal of the American Medical Association, how much did large publicly-traded health companies pay out to shareholders from 2001 to 2022?
(A) $5.9tn
(B) $160,000
(C) $25bn
(D) $8,000
(E) Over $2.6tn
explanation: The article states: "Large publicly-traded health companies have tripled their profits over the last two decades, paying out shareholders over $2.6tn from 2001 to 2022, according to a study in the Journal of the American Medical Association."
model_output:
According to the study published in JAMA Internal Medicine (part of the JAMA Network), large publicly-traded health care companies paid out a total of $2.60 trillion to shareholders from 2001 to 2022[[1]](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamainternalmedicine/articlepdf/2829736/jamainternal_roy_2025_ld_240038_1743791987.60122.pdf?resultClick=1). Some secondary reports on the study refer to this as "at least $2.6 trillion"[[2]](https://www.facebook.com/senatorsanders/posts/the-top-health-care-companies-in-america-spent-95-of-their-profits-to-make-their/1206231070872368), which aligns most closely with option (E) given the phrasing and the available choices.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>
